mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or forward returns are not significantly positive during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds the 235.0 threshold, anticipated monetary tightening compresses equity multiples, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 235.0, or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: The joint expansion of total employment and simultaneous decline in the unemployment rate signals robust labor market tightening, which supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regressions, or the combined signal fails to outperform single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a supply-constrained regime indicates bottleneck resolution and margin expansion, producing positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative during supply-constrained periods, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical edge (t-stat < 1.5) when INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth combined with elevated housing starts reflects sustained aggregate demand, which lifts corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in > 60% of high-GDP-growth quarters, or the signal is fully subsumed by monetary policy expectations.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of near-zero policy rates and elevated inflation creates a stagflationary risk premium that triggers immediate equity sell-offs, producing negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat during this specific rate-inflation configuration, or the interaction effect vanishes in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration and recession risk, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in > 50% of instances following a 0.4pp unemployment jump, or the signal shows zero predictive accuracy in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising retail sales and stagnant industrial production indicates supply chain friction that compresses margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative returns, or margin compression does not materialize in earnings reports within the 20-day window.
